---
title: "2. Defining a FAIR brain network model"
subtitle: "Sessions 2.1 · 2.2"
toc: false
---

In this chapter you will use the TVB Ontology (TVB-O) to define a neural mass
model in a FAIR-compliant way, simulate a single node, and then connect
multiple nodes into a whole-brain network driven by structural connectivity.

## Sessions

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [2.1 Mean field models](02a_mean_field_models.qmd)
Load a neural mass model from the TVB-O knowledge base. Inspect parameters and
state variables. Simulate a single uncoupled node and visualise its dynamics.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [2.2 Coupled networks](02b_coupled_networks.qmd)
Connect multiple neural masses via a structural connectome. Understand how
coupling functions and long-range delays shape collective network activity.
:::

:::

---

*See the [workshop agenda](../agenda/agenda.qmd).*
